name: Military Intelligence Systems Maintainer/Integrator
description: Maintains, integrates, and repairs military intelligence systems, including electronic warfare equipment, computers, networks, sensors, and surveillance platforms. Performs system diagnostics, troubleshooting, hardware/software repairs, and ensures operational readiness of intelligence collection assets. Works across tactical and strategic environments to support mission-critical intelligence operations.

name: Cyber Network Defender
description: Protects and defends Army networks and information systems against cyber threats. Conducts monitoring, incident response, vulnerability management, and implements security architecture changes to maintain mission assurance.
summarized_description: Army 25D Cyber Network Defenders secure and monitor enterprise networks, manage vulnerabilities, and respond to security incidents. Suited for SOC, network security, and infrastructure protection roles in MSPs, large integrators, and public-sector security teams.

name: Data Systems Administrator
description: Installs, configures, and maintains Marine Corps servers, applications, and data services. Ensures availability, performance, and security of systems supporting command and control in garrison and deployed environments.
summarized_description: Marine Corps 0671 Data Systems Administrators manage servers and applications that support command and control, often in austere environments. Their hands-on experience is a strong match for systems admin, IT operations, and field systems support roles.

name: Information Technology Specialist
description: Installs, configures, and maintains Army computer networks and communications systems supporting voice, data, and video services. Provides user support, troubleshoots network outages, and ensures secure, reliable connectivity in garrison and deployed environments.
summarized_description: Army 25B Information Technology Specialists manage and support military computer networks, servers, and end-user devices to keep voice and data communications running. They handle installation, configuration, troubleshooting, and user support in both garrison and deployed environments, mapping well to civilian IT support and junior network admin roles.

name: Cyber Operations Specialist
description: Conducts offensive and defensive cyberspace operations to protect Army networks and degrade adversary capabilities. Performs vulnerability assessments, intrusion analysis, and supports mission planning with cyber effects integration.
summarized_description: Army 17C Cyber Operations Specialists execute offensive and defensive cyber missions, including vulnerability assessments, intrusion detection, exploitation, and forensics. Ideal for roles in cyber defense, red teaming, incident response, and advanced security engineering within government and defense-focused companies.

name: Cyber Operations Officer
description: Leads teams conducting full-spectrum cyberspace operations, integrating offensive, defensive, and information operations with broader mission plans. Manages cyber mission forces, resources, and joint coordination with interagency partners.
recommended_certs_contract: Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control (CRISC)

growth: Harvard Cybersecurity: Managing Risk in the Information Age

summarized_description: Army 17A Cyber Operations Officers lead and plan full-spectrum cyberspace missions, integrating offensive, defensive, and information operations. They manage cyber teams, resources, and risk while coordinating with joint and interagency partners to deliver cyber effects in support of operational and strategic objectives.

name: Cryptologic Technician - Networks
description: Performs computer network operations in support of signals intelligence and cyberspace missions. Conducts network exploitation, analysis, and defensive measures against advanced cyber threats targeting Navy and joint networks.
summarized_description: Navy CTNs conduct advanced network exploitation and cyber operations in highly classified environments. Their skills in packet analysis, malware, and threat intel are a strong match for high-end cyber defense, red teaming, and security research roles in intelligence-focused organizations.

name: Cyber Systems Operations
description: Administers and secures Air Force servers, applications, and core infrastructure services. Performs system installation, configuration, patching, and troubleshooting to ensure mission-critical availability and security.
summarized_description: Air Force 3D0X2 Cyber Systems Operations specialists manage servers, virtualization, and core services for secure Air Force networks. This background aligns with systems admin, infrastructure, and cloud operations roles in enterprise and government IT environments.

name: Cyber Warfare Engineer
description: Designs, develops, and deploys specialized cyber capabilities and tools for naval and joint operations. Provides deep technical expertise in software engineering, cryptography, and computer network operations to support cyber mission forces.
recommended_certs_contract: GIAC Exploit Researcher and Advanced Pen Tester (GXPN)

starter: Secure Coding in C and C++

summarized_description: Navy Cyber Warfare Engineers are highly technical officers who build and integrate advanced cyber tools and capabilities. They apply secure software engineering, cryptography, and deep protocol knowledge to develop offensive and defensive solutions for some of the most sensitive cyber missions in the fleet.

name: Cyberspace Officer
description: Plans, coordinates, and commands Marine Corps cyberspace operations, integrating cyber effects with maneuver, fires, and information operations. Oversees cyber mission teams, risk management, and capability development.
summarized_description: Marine Corps 1702 Cyberspace Officers command and coordinate cyber forces, integrating cyber effects with broader Marine Air-Ground operations. They manage cyber risk, policy, and programs while leading mission planning and execution for complex, multi-domain cyberspace operations.

name: Network Administrator
description: Installs, configures, and maintains Marine Corps tactical and garrison networks. Ensures availability, performance, and security of routers, switches, and network services supporting combat and support units.
summarized_description: Marine Corps 0631 Network Administrators build and maintain tactical and garrison networks that connect combat and support units. They configure routers, switches, and encryption devices, troubleshoot connectivity in austere environments, and apply baseline security controls to keep communications flowing.

name: Cyber Operations Officer
description: Leads cyber defense and mission assurance for Space Force networks and space mission systems. Integrates cyber risk management, policy, and operations, and coordinates with joint and commercial partners to protect critical space infrastructure.

starter: DoD Space and Cyber Policy Courses

summarized_description: Space Force 17S0 Cyber Operations Officers manage cyber risk and mission assurance for networks and space mission systems. They lead cyber teams, oversee compliance and policy, and coordinate with government and commercial partners to secure critical space infrastructure from evolving cyber threats.

name: Information Systems Technician
description: Provides installation, maintenance, and support for Coast Guard communications and information systems, including networks, servers, and radios. Delivers help desk services, performs system administration, and ensures secure connectivity for cutters, stations, and command centers.
summarized_description: Coast Guard ITs support networks, servers, radios, and end-user systems for cutters, stations, and command centers, blending system administration with communications support and help desk services. Their broad IT and comms background matches sysadmin, IT support, and junior network roles in maritime, public sector, and MSP environments.

name: Intelligence Analyst
description: Collects, processes, and analyzes multi-discipline intelligence to produce assessments that support commanders’ decision-making. Fuses human, signals, and imagery reporting into actionable products for tactical and operational missions.
summarized_description: All-source intelligence analyst experienced in fusing HUMINT, SIGINT, and GEOINT to produce tactical and operational assessments. Skilled in structured analytic techniques, intelligence preparation of the battlefield, and briefing commanders. Strong fit for defense intel, risk, and threat analysis roles.

name: Operations Specialist (C4ISR Focus)
description: Operates command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (C4ISR) systems supporting Coast Guard missions. Monitors networks, sensors, and data feeds for maritime domain awareness with a cyber-aware focus.
summarized_description: Coast Guard OS specialists with a C4ISR focus operate command center systems that fuse sensor, communications, and network data for maritime missions. They monitor feeds and basic cyber indicators, log incidents, and support real-time operations in secure, watch-floor environments.

name: Fusion Analyst
description: Conducts multi-INT fusion of SIGINT, GEOINT, MASINT, and cyber data to generate actionable intelligence. Supports time-sensitive operations, targeting, and battlespace awareness for joint and coalition forces.
summarized_description: Air Force 1N4X1 Fusion Analysts integrate SIGINT, GEOINT, MASINT, and cyber data to produce high-value intelligence for targeting and battlespace awareness. Operating in SCIF and operations centers, they support time-sensitive missions and are well aligned with fusion, CTI, and data-focused intelligence roles in the defense industry.

name: Intelligence Officer
description: Leads intelligence operations and analytic teams supporting air, space, and cyber missions. Manages collection, production, and dissemination while advising commanders on threat capabilities, vulnerabilities, and courses of action.
summarized_description: Air Force 14N Intelligence Officers oversee intelligence units supporting air, space, and cyber operations. They direct collection, analysis, and dissemination, provide senior-level threat assessments, and commonly transition into program management, senior analyst, and strategy roles within defense contractors and federal agencies.

name: Intelligence Specialist
description: Collects, analyzes, and disseminates intelligence to support maritime law enforcement, counter-narcotics, and homeland security operations. Produces threat assessments, vessel profiles, and intelligence reports for Coast Guard units and partners.

growth: Homeland Security and Emergency Management certificate

summarized_description: Coast Guard intelligence specialist focused on maritime, law enforcement, and homeland security intelligence. Experienced in OSINT, vessel tracking, and threat assessments for counter-narcotics and security missions. Strong candidate for LE intel, homeland security, and maritime analysis roles.

name: Signal Support Systems Specialist
description: Provides signal support for the command and control of Army units, including radios, tactical data networks, and battlefield communications systems. Installs, maintains, and troubleshoots voice and data systems in field and deployed environments.
summarized_description: Army 25U Signal Support Systems Specialists operate and maintain tactical radios, satellite links, and voice/data systems that connect units in the field. With strong hands-on experience in radio, cabling, and field troubleshooting, they align well to telecom field tech, radio technician, and wireless communications support roles in both defense and commercial sectors.

name: Information Services Technician
description: Manages and engineers Army information services and communications architectures at the unit and brigade level. Oversees network design, configuration control, cybersecurity compliance, and integration of tactical and strategic communications systems.
summarized_description: Army 255A Information Services Technicians lead the design, implementation, and governance of tactical and enterprise communications networks. They blend hands-on engineering with IT project management and cybersecurity compliance, making them ideal for network engineer, infrastructure architect, and IT operations leadership roles supporting defense and large enterprises.

name: Electronics Technician (Communications)
description: Maintains and repairs complex electronic communications systems, including radios, satellite terminals, and cryptographic equipment. Conducts preventative maintenance, fault isolation, and alignment to keep shipboard and shore communications fully mission capable.
summarized_description: Navy Electronics Technicians (Communications) service and repair shipboard radios, satellite terminals, and other communications electronics using advanced test equipment and technical manuals. Their hands-on RF and electronics maintenance experience aligns with field service, broadcast, and telecom maintenance roles.

name: Information Warfare Systems Engineer
description: Oversees integration and engineering of Navy information warfare and communications systems across platforms. Provides technical leadership for complex C4I architectures, interoperability, and modernization efforts.
summarized_description: Navy 6330 Information Warfare Systems Engineers lead design and integration of complex C4I and communications systems across ships and shore facilities. They apply systems engineering, network design, and program leadership to ensure interoperable, secure communications, fitting advanced roles in C4ISR engineering, solutions architecture, and technical program management.

name: Cyber Effects Operations Officer (Communications Focus)
description: Leads planning and employment of Air Force cyber and communications capabilities in support of operations. Oversees secure network architectures, command and control communications, and integration with joint cyber and space assets.
summarized_description: Air Force 17S3 Cyber Effects Operations Officers lead secure communications and cyber operations planning for Air Force and joint missions. With expertise in secure architectures, policy, and mission planning, they map to cyber operations manager, IT program manager, and senior cybersecurity leadership roles in the defense and federal sectors.

name: Field Radio Operator
description: Operates and maintains tactical radios and communications equipment to provide voice and limited data links for Marine units in the field. Establishes comm plans, sets up antennas, and ensures reliable connectivity under austere and combat conditions.
summarized_description: Marine Corps 0621 Field Radio Operators provide tactical voice and limited data communications for units using HF/VHF/UHF radios and field antennas in austere environments. Their experience with land mobile radio systems and field troubleshooting suits public safety, wireless, and radio communications technician roles.

name: Communications Officer
description: Commands and manages Marine Corps communications units and network assets. Plans, coordinates, and supervises employment of voice, data, and satellite systems to support maneuver and logistics operations.
summarized_description: Marine Corps 0602 Communications Officers lead units that deliver voice, data, and SATCOM services to support maneuver and logistics. They combine technical understanding of tactical networks with project management and leadership, mapping well to IT manager, network operations manager, and communications program leadership roles.

name: Electronics Technician (Communications)
description: Maintains and repairs Coast Guard communications, navigation, and surveillance electronics. Ensures reliable operation of radios, radar, and satellite systems aboard cutters, small boats, and shore facilities.
summarized_description: Coast Guard Communications ETs maintain radios, radar, SATCOM, and navigation electronics that support maritime safety and operations. Their strong electronics troubleshooting and RF experience align with marine electronics tech, RF technician, and radar maintenance roles for OEMs, shipyards, and defense contractors.

name: Information Protection Technician
description: Serves as the technical expert for information assurance and cybersecurity programs across Army organizations. Designs security architectures, oversees risk management, and advises commanders on cyber readiness and compliance.
summarized_description: Army 255S Information Protection Technicians lead cybersecurity and RMF efforts, design secure architectures, and manage compliance across complex networks. Well aligned to ISSO/ISSM, security architect, and GRC leadership roles in federal and defense contractor environments.

name: Cyber Warfare Operations
description: Executes offensive and defensive cyber warfare missions to support combatant commands. Performs mission planning, network exploitation, defensive countermeasures, and real-time operations in highly classified environments.
summarized_description: Air Force 1B4X1 Cyber Warfare Operators conduct offensive and defensive cyber missions, integrating with combat operations. Their expertise supports roles in advanced cyber operations, red/blue teaming, and high-end SOC engineering within cleared defense environments.

name: Cyber Warfare Operations Officer
description: Commands and directs cyber warfare units conducting full-spectrum cyberspace operations. Develops tactics, plans missions, manages risk, and synchronizes cyber effects with joint and coalition operations.
summarized_description: Air Force 17S Cyber Warfare Officers lead cyber units, align operations with joint missions, and manage risk and strategy. They transition well into SOC leadership, cyber program management, and director-level security roles in large enterprises and defense contractors.

name: Cyberspace Warfare Operator
description: Conducts offensive and defensive cyberspace operations in support of Marine Air-Ground Task Force and joint missions. Performs network exploitation, defense, and incident response within specialized cyber units.
summarized_description: Marine 1721 Cyberspace Warfare Operators execute offensive and defensive cyber missions, including pen testing, threat hunting, and incident response. This maps directly to SOC, red/blue team, and cyber engineer roles supporting DoD and critical infrastructure.

name: Cyberspace Warfare Officer
description: Plans, directs, and integrates cyberspace operations for Marine Corps and joint missions. Leads cyber units, develops tactics and training, and synchronizes cyber effects with kinetic and information operations.
summarized_description: Marine 1720 Cyberspace Warfare Officers lead and integrate cyber capabilities across Marine and joint operations. Their background supports transition into cyber leadership, governance, and program management roles in large enterprises and federal agencies.

name: Maritime Enforcement Specialist (Cyber Support)
description: Specializes in cyber aspects of maritime law enforcement and port security. Supports investigations involving digital evidence, networked systems aboard vessels, and cyber risk to critical maritime infrastructure.
summarized_description: Coast Guard ME (Cyber Support) personnel bridge maritime law enforcement and cyber, handling digital evidence and cyber risk to ports and vessels. They are suited for junior digital forensics, cybercrime support, and OSINT/compliance roles in government and regulated industries.

name: Space Systems Operations (Satellite Communications)
description: Operates and monitors satellite communications payloads and ground systems that support global military communications. Performs command and control actions, anomaly detection, and coordination with network and mission partners to ensure continuous, secure SATCOM services.
summarized_description: Space Force 1C6X1 SATCOM-focused operators run satellite payloads and ground systems that provide global military communications, performing console operations, anomaly response, and coordination with network partners. Their experience fits satellite operations technician, NOC analyst, and mission operations roles in space and telecom companies.

name: Signals Intelligence Analyst
description: Intercepts, analyzes, and reports foreign communications and electronic signals to support targeting and situational awareness. Works closely with collection assets to optimize signals exploitation and produce time-sensitive intelligence.
summarized_description: Signals intelligence analyst specializing in interception and analysis of foreign communications and electronic emissions. Experienced with tactical SIGINT systems, time-sensitive reporting, and target development. Well-suited for SIGINT, cyber threat, and network traffic analysis roles in the defense sector.

name: Cryptologic Technician Interpretive
description: Provides foreign language translation and analysis of intercepted communications to support cryptologic and intelligence operations. Produces detailed reports and time-sensitive tips for fleet and national consumers.
summarized_description: Cryptologic linguist specializing in foreign language exploitation of communications for SIGINT and intelligence missions. Combines language skills, cultural context, and analytic tradecraft to produce reports and time-sensitive tips. Ideal for intel linguist and SIGINT analyst roles requiring TS/SCI and language proficiency.

name: Intelligence Specialist
description: Collects and analyzes intelligence information to support naval and joint operations at sea and ashore. Produces threat assessments, mission briefs, and imagery products for strike planning and maritime security.
summarized_description: Navy intelligence specialist focused on maritime and all-source analysis, imagery exploitation, and mission briefing. Experienced supporting fleet and joint operations from shipboard and shore-based SCIFs. Strong match for maritime intel, GEOINT, and threat analysis positions requiring a clearance.

name: Intelligence Officer
description: Plans, directs, and manages intelligence activities supporting Air Force and joint operations. Leads teams conducting analysis, targeting, and collection management while advising commanders on threat and risk.
recommended_certs_contract: Leadership in Cyber and Intelligence certificate

growth: Executive Program in National Security Studies

summarized_description: Air Force intelligence officer with experience managing intel teams, targeting, and collection for air and joint operations. Strong leadership, risk assessment, and program management background. Ideal for senior intel manager, program lead, and strategic threat intelligence roles requiring TS/SCI.

name: Network Intelligence Analyst
description: Conducts intelligence analysis on digital networks and communications infrastructure to support cyberspace and signals operations. Identifies adversary capabilities, vulnerabilities, and activity patterns across global networks.
summarized_description: Digital network intelligence analyst specializing in cyber and SIGINT-focused analysis of global networks. Skilled in traffic analysis, threat intelligence, and technical reporting within SCIF, NOC, and SOC environments. Well-suited for CTI, network security, and threat hunting roles with cleared defense employers.

name: Intelligence Officer
description: Leads and manages intelligence operations for Navy and joint commands, directing collection, analysis, and dissemination activities. Advises commanders on adversary capabilities, intentions, and risk to maritime operations.

growth: Strategic Intelligence and National Security Studies

summarized_description: Navy intelligence officer with experience leading intel teams, managing collection and production, and advising maritime commanders. Strong background in all-source fusion, operational planning, and program management. Fits senior analyst, intel manager, and program lead roles needing TS/SCI and leadership experience.

name: Space Operations (Intelligence Focus)
description: Supports space operations by providing intelligence-driven situational awareness on space, cyber, and electromagnetic environments. Monitors threats to space assets and contributes to defensive and offensive space operational planning.
summarized_description: Space operations specialist with an intelligence focus, providing threat analysis and situational awareness for space and cyber domains. Experienced with space domain awareness systems and joint operations centers. Well-suited for space intel, SDA, and mission operations analysis roles with cleared space contractors.

name: Targeting Analyst (Space)
description: Conducts targeting analysis focused on space and counterspace capabilities, integrating multi-domain intelligence to support space operations. Develops target system models, vulnerability assessments, and recommended courses of action.

starter: Intro to Space Operations and Intelligence

summarized_description: Space-focused targeting analyst integrating multi-domain intelligence to assess space and counterspace systems. Skilled in target system modeling, vulnerability analysis, and mission planning support for space operations. Strong match for space intel, targeting, and threat analysis roles in the national security space sector.

name: Intelligence Specialist
description: Collects, analyzes, and disseminates intelligence to support Marine Air-Ground Task Force operations. Produces threat assessments, terrain analysis, and targeting products for expeditionary missions.
summarized_description: Marine Corps intelligence specialist focused on all-source analysis for expeditionary ground operations. Experienced in IPB, targeting support, and field-deployed intelligence production using DCGS-MC and GEOINT tools. Strong fit for defense intel, GEOINT, and threat analysis roles with operational focus.

name: Operations Intelligence
description: Performs and manages intelligence activities to support Air Force and joint flying operations. Prepares mission briefs, target materials, and threat assessments for aircrews and operations centers.
summarized_description: Air Force operations intelligence analyst supporting aircrews and operations centers with threat assessments, target materials, and mission briefs. Experienced in air order of battle, mission planning tools, and time-sensitive reporting. Strong candidate for aviation-focused intel and operations analysis roles.

name: Cyber Transport Systems
description: Installs, configures, and maintains Air Force voice, data, and video transport systems, including routers, switches, and cryptographic devices. Ensures reliable, secure network connectivity for mission systems across base, air, and deployed environments.
summarized_description: Air Force 3D1X2 Cyber Transport specialists design, install, and maintain secure voice, data, and video transport networks using enterprise routers, switches, and crypto gear. Their background in routing, switching, and unified communications fits network engineer and NOC engineer positions supporting DoD and commercial infrastructure.

name: Cyber Operations Officer (Network Operations)
description: Leads planning, integration, and defense of Air Force communications and cyber infrastructure, focusing on network operations and mission assurance. Oversees teams managing base-level and enterprise networks, ensuring compliance, risk management, and continuity of operations.
recommended_certs_contract: Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control (CRISC)
summarized_description: Air Force 17D3 Cyber Operations Officers lead network operations and cyber defense for Air Force communications infrastructure, managing teams, risk, and large-scale IT initiatives. Their leadership and governance experience aligns with IT manager, cyber operations manager, and infrastructure director roles in complex enterprise or DoD environments.

name: Satellite Communications (SATCOM) Operator
description: Operates, maintains, and troubleshoots Marine Corps satellite communications terminals supporting long-haul voice and data links. Conducts satellite access requests, aligns antennas, and ensures secure, reliable connectivity between tactical and strategic networks.
summarized_description: Marine Corps 0627 SATCOM Operators run and maintain tactical satellite terminals that provide long-haul connectivity between deployed forces and higher headquarters. Their mix of RF, satellite, and basic networking skills aligns with VSAT technician, teleports operator, and satellite field engineer roles in defense and commercial sectors.

name: Information Professional Officer
description: Leads the planning, operation, and defense of Navy information systems and cyber infrastructure. Oversees networks, cybersecurity programs, and communications capabilities that support fleet operations worldwide.
summarized_description: Navy Information Professional Officers manage large-scale networks and cybersecurity programs that enable fleet operations. Their blend of leadership, RMF knowledge, and IT operations experience maps to IT and cyber management, program leadership, and senior security roles.

name: All-Source Intelligence Technician
description: Serves as the technical expert and integrator for all-source intelligence operations at battalion through division level. Oversees production, dissemination, and quality control of intelligence products and manages analyst teams.
summarized_description: Warrant officer-level all-source intelligence technician with experience leading fusion cells, managing collection and production, and advising commanders. Strong fit for senior intel analyst, operations manager, and program analyst roles requiring TS/SCI and team leadership in defense environments.

name: Multichannel Transmission Systems Operator-Maintainer
description: Operates and maintains line-of-sight and satellite multichannel communications systems that carry command and control, voice, and data traffic. Ensures tactical radio and transport equipment is properly configured, aligned, and safeguarded to support continuous connectivity.
summarized_description: Army 25Q Multichannel Transmission Operators install, operate, and maintain tactical radio and satellite transport systems that carry mission-critical voice and data. Their field-focused RF and satellite experience translates to telecom, RF, and satellite field technician roles supporting commercial and government networks.

name: Information Systems Technician
description: Manages shipboard and shore-based Navy communications networks, including secure voice, data, and messaging systems. Performs system administration, cybersecurity compliance, and help desk support to ensure reliable connectivity for command and control.
summarized_description: Navy ITs administer and secure shipboard and shore-based networks, servers, and messaging systems that enable Navy communications worldwide. They combine network operations, system administration, and user support, making them strong candidates for NOC, sysadmin, and infrastructure roles in defense and commercial environments.

name: Geospatial Intelligence Specialist
description: Produces and analyzes geospatial intelligence products, including maps, terrain studies, and imagery-based assessments, to support Marine operations. Integrates GEOINT with other intelligence disciplines for planning and targeting.
summarized_description: Marine GEOINT specialist experienced in geospatial and imagery analysis, terrain studies, and map production using ArcGIS and DCGS-MC. Supports planning and targeting with GEOINT-driven products. Ideal for GEOINT, GIS, and imagery analysis roles in defense and geospatial industries.

name: RF Transmission Systems
description: Operates and maintains radio frequency, line-of-sight, and satellite communications systems supporting Air Force missions. Installs, aligns, and troubleshoots RF equipment, antennas, and associated baseband communications hardware.
summarized_description: Air Force 3D1X3 RF Transmission airmen operate and maintain RF, microwave, and satellite communications systems that enable long-range connectivity. Their RF theory, antenna, and field troubleshooting skills position them well for RF technician, SATCOM field tech, and wireless communications roles with telecoms and defense contractors.
